compiled_state.author: Iso Tester

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Recurred 2026-07-03 on Gerrit change 174 (sync.remote), CI run https://github.com/navapbc/rebar/actions/runs/28632056833 — CI (macos-latest, py3.12), default suite: test_graph_build_1000_tickets_under_2s took 2.084s for a 1,000-ticket chain (limit 2.0s). ~4% over on a loaded macOS runner; unrelated to the change. Cast Verified-1; cleared with 'recheck'. More evidence the absolute 2.0s wall-clock limit is too tight for GitHub-hosted macOS runners.

compiled_state.description:
## Reproduction Steps

1. Open a PR; CI runs the default pytest suite across the matrix (ubuntu/macos × py3.11/3.12).
2. On a loaded runner (observed on `macos-latest, py3.12`), `tests/scripts/graph/test_graph_perf.py` fails on absolute wall-clock assertions even though the code is unchanged.

Observed failures (PR #108 run 28505746020):
- `test_graph_perf.py:59` — `build_dep_graph took 3.192s for 1,000-ticket chain (limit: 2.0s)`
- `test_graph_perf.py:327` — `9 cross-tier add_dependency calls took 8.65s (limit: 5.0s)`

Both passed on all ubuntu runners in the same PR; a manual re-run of the macOS job passed. So the failures are runner-load-dependent, not a code regression.

## Expected vs Actual

- **Expected:** perf tests gate against genuine algorithmic regressions and are stable across CI runners.
- **Actual:** they assert against fixed absolute-time thresholds (`< 2.0s`, `< 5.0s`) that a slow/loaded shared runner (esp. macOS) blows past, producing false-failure PR blocks that require manual re-runs.

## Impact

Flaky red on unrelated PRs → wasted re-runs + eroded trust in the gate (a green PR gets blocked by an infra hiccup). This blocked epic 3156's final merge (PR #108) until a manual re-run.

## Root cause

Absolute wall-clock budgets are sensitive to shared-runner contention. A perf test should measure a *relative* signal (scaling / ratio vs a baseline op on the same machine) or run in an isolated, non-gating, retried context — not a raw seconds ceiling.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `tests/scripts/graph/test_graph_perf.py` no longer false-fails under runner load: either (a) assert on a RELATIVE metric (e.g. per-op time scaled to a same-machine baseline, or big-O scaling ratio between N and 2N) instead of an absolute seconds ceiling, or (b) mark the wall-clock assertions with a bounded retry (e.g. `flaky`/rerun) AND widen the ceilings with generous headroom, or (c) move the raw-timing assertions to a dedicated NON-gating perf job (still run, reported, not merge-blocking) while keeping a correctness-only assertion in the gating suite.
- [ ] The chosen approach still catches a real >2x algorithmic regression (a deliberately O(n^2) reintroduction fails the test) — documented in the test.
- [ ] No absolute single-run wall-clock ceiling remains in the gating path for these two tests.
compiled_state.title: Flaky graph perf tests (absolute wall-clock limits) fail on loaded CI runners
